name: dashboard-designer
description: Mock flow for the ADK Dashboard Designer agent. Each chip fires two LLM calls: (1) initial user message → tool_call updateDashboard, then (2) post-tool LLM call → final assistant text. Fixtures are arranged in PAIRS, with the tool-response fixture FIRST so it matches the post-tool call (last msg role:tool with matching toolCallId), and the chip fixture SECOND so it matches the initial user msg (the tool-response fixture's last-is-tool gate prevents it from matching here). DO NOT add `hasToolResult: false` to the chip fixtures — after the first chip click, every subsequent chip's initial LLM call has a tool message in history, and that gate would skip the fixture, falling through to zz-catchall. The userMessage substrings are specific enough to disambiguate between chips on their own.

response.content: Sarah's work is up — three open, mix of high and med.

toolCalls.name: updateDashboard
toolCalls.arguments: {"filter":{"assignee":"Sarah"},"focus":"Showing every issue Sarah owns across all statuses and priorities."}

response.content: Three urgent — Safari payment, Postgres pool, and Slack dedupe. Two are already in flight.

match.userMessage: Filter the dashboard to just the urgent

toolCalls.name: updateDashboard
toolCalls.arguments: {"filter":{"priority":"Urgent"},"focus":"Only urgent-priority issues — the ones to unblock first."}

response.content: Alex is the heaviest at 5, then Sarah and Jordan at 4-5 each. Two issues are unassigned.

match.userMessage: Break down the dashboard by assignee

toolCalls.name: updateDashboard
toolCalls.arguments: {"filter":{},"focus":"Full backlog broken down by who owns what — Alex is carrying the most."}

response.content: Four in flight, with two urgent and one high — payments and infra dominate.

match.userMessage: Show me everything currently in progress

toolCalls.name: updateDashboard
toolCalls.arguments: {"filter":{"status":"In Progress"},"focus":"In-flight work only — what the team is actively shipping this week."}
